---
title: "Asymptotics Visualization for Longitudinal TMLE under an LMTP"
---

```{r, include = FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  collapse = TRUE,
  comment = "#>"
)
```

```{r setup}
# library(modulatR)
devtools::load_all(".")
library(TargetedLearning)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(purrr)
library(scales)

theme_set(theme_minimal(base_size = 13))
set.seed(2026)
```

Simulation setup

```{r}
true_diff <- 2.49

generate_data <- function(n) {
  W  <- rnorm(n)
  L1 <- 0.5 * W + rnorm(n)
  A1 <- 0.6 * W + 0.8 * L1 + rnorm(n)
  L2 <- 0.4 * W + 0.7 * A1 + 0.6 * L1 + rnorm(n)
  A2 <- 0.3 * W + 0.5 * L2 + rnorm(n)
  Y  <- 1 + 0.5 * W + 0.4 * L1 + 0.3 * L2 + 1.2 * A1 + 0.8 * A2 + rnorm(n)

  data.frame(W = W, L1 = L1, A1 = A1, L2 = L2, A2 = A2, Y = Y)
}

make_ds <- function(df) {
  LMTPData$new(
    data = df,
    A_cols = c("A1", "A2"),
    L_cols = list("L1", "L2"),
    W_cols = "W",
    Y_col = "Y"
  )
}

make_nuisance <- function(policy_seq) {
  LMTPNuisanceFactory$new(
    learners_g = nadir::lnr_glm_density,
    policy_seq = policy_seq,
    A_type = "continuous",
    g_mode = "density",
    cross_fit = FALSE
  )
}

make_fluctuation <- function() {
  LMTPFluctuationSubmodel$new(
    family = stats::gaussian(),
    use_intercept = FALSE
  )
}
```

```{r}
# to determine truth: 
n <- 1e6
W <- rnorm(n)
eL1 <- rnorm(n)
eA1 <- rnorm(n)
eL2 <- rnorm(n)
eA2 <- rnorm(n)
eY  <- rnorm(n)

L1 <- 0.5 * W + eL1
A1 <- 0.6 * W + 0.8 * L1 + eA1
L2 <- 0.4 * W + 0.7 * A1 + 0.6 * L1 + eL2
A2 <- 0.3 * W + 0.5 * L2 + eA2
Y  <- 1 + 0.5 * W + 0.4 * L1 + 0.3 * L2 + 1.2 * A1 + 0.8 * A2 + eY

A1d <- A1 + 1
L2d <- 0.4 * W + 0.7 * A1d + 0.6 * L1 + eL2
A2d <- (0.3 * W + 0.5 * L2d + eA2) + 1
Yd  <- 1 + 0.5 * W + 0.4 * L1 + 0.3 * L2d + 1.2 * A1d + 0.8 * A2d + eY

true_diff <- mean(Yd - Y)



# dgp 

generate_data <- function(n) {
  W  <- rnorm(n)
  L1 <- 0.5 * W + rnorm(n)
  A1 <- 0.6 * W + 0.8 * L1 + rnorm(n)
  L2 <- 0.4 * W + 0.7 * A1 + 0.6 * L1 + rnorm(n)
  A2 <- 0.3 * W + 0.5 * L2 + rnorm(n)
  Y  <- 1 + 0.5 * W + 0.4 * L1 + 0.3 * L2 + 1.2 * A1 + 0.8 * A2 + rnorm(n)

  data.frame(W = W, L1 = L1, A1 = A1, L2 = L2, A2 = A2, Y = Y)
}

make_ds <- function(df) {
  LMTPData$new(
    data = df,
    A_cols = c("A1", "A2"),
    L_cols = list("L1", "L2"),
    W_cols = "W",
    Y_col = "Y"
  )
}

make_nuisance <- function(policy_seq) {
  LMTPNuisanceFactory$new(
    learners_g = nadir::lnr_glm_density,
    policy_seq = policy_seq,
    A_type = "continuous",
    g_mode = "density",
    cross_fit = FALSE
  )
}

make_fluctuation <- function() {
  LMTPFluctuationSubmodel$new(
    family = stats::gaussian(),
    use_intercept = FALSE
  )
}
```


One replicate of the simulation:

```{r}
one_rep <- function(n) {
  df <- generate_data(n)
  ds <- make_ds(df)

  policy_seq <- repeat_policy_over_time(
    mtp_additive_shift(delta = 1),
    tau = ds$tau()
  )

  identity_seq <- identity_policy(
    A_type = "continuous",
    tau = ds$tau()
  )

  nf_shift <- make_nuisance(policy_seq)
  nf_identity <- make_nuisance(identity_seq)
  fluct <- make_fluctuation()

  fit_shift <- make_lmtp_tl_task(
    ds = ds,
    policy_seq = policy_seq,
    nuisance_factory = nf_shift,
    fluctuation = fluct,
    learners_Q = stats::glm
  )$run()

  fit_identity <- make_lmtp_tl_task(
    ds = ds,
    policy_seq = identity_seq,
    nuisance_factory = nf_identity,
    fluctuation = fluct,
    learners_Q = stats::glm
  )$run()

  fit_diff <- lmtp_delta_contrast(
    fit1 = fit_shift,
    fit0 = fit_identity,
    transform = "difference"
  )

  tibble(
    estimate = fit_diff$estimate,
    se = fit_diff$se,
    ci_lower = fit_diff$ci["lower"],
    ci_upper = fit_diff$ci["upper"],
    covered = as.numeric(fit_diff$ci["lower"] <= true_diff &&
                           true_diff <= fit_diff$ci["upper"])
  )
}
```


Monte Carlo grid:

```{r}
run_grid <- function(n, B = 200) {
  reps <- map_dfr(seq_len(B), ~ one_rep(n))

  tibble(
    n = n,
    B = B,
    mc_mean = mean(reps$estimate),
    bias = mean(reps$estimate) - true_diff,
    empirical_sd = sd(reps$estimate),
    mean_estimated_se = mean(reps$se),
    coverage_95 = mean(reps$covered),
    se_to_sd_ratio = mean(reps$se) / sd(reps$estimate)
  )
}

# n_grid <- c(1000, 2500, 5000, 10000, 20000, 50000)
n_grid <- round(cumsum(rep(sqrt(300), 6))^2, 0)

sim_summary <- map_dfr(n_grid, run_grid, B = 3000)
sim_summary
```

Visualization

```{r}
p_bias <- ggplot(sim_summary, aes(x = n, y = bias)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = 2) +
  geom_line() +
  geom_point(size = 2) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = n_grid) +
  labs(
    title = "Bias across sample size",
    x = "n",
    y = "Monte Carlo bias"
  )

sd_df <- sim_summary %>%
  select(n, empirical_sd, mean_estimated_se) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = c(empirical_sd, mean_estimated_se),
               names_to = "quantity", values_to = "value")

p_sd <- ggplot(sd_df, aes(x = n, y = value, group = quantity, linetype = quantity)) +
  geom_line() +
  geom_point(size = 2) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = n_grid) +
  labs(
    title = "Empirical SD vs mean estimated SE",
    x = "n",
    y = "Value",
    linetype = NULL
  )

p_cov <- ggplot(sim_summary, aes(x = n, y = coverage_95)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0.95, linetype = 2) +
  geom_line() +
  geom_point(size = 2) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = n_grid) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = percent_format(accuracy = 1), limits = c(0.8, 1.0)) +
  labs(
    title = "95% CI coverage",
    x = "n",
    y = "Coverage"
  )

p_ratio <- ggplot(sim_summary, aes(x = n, y = se_to_sd_ratio)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 1, linetype = 2) +
  geom_line() +
  geom_point(size = 2) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = n_grid) +
  labs(
    title = "Mean estimated SE / empirical SD",
    x = "n",
    y = "Ratio"
  )

p_bias
p_sd
p_cov
p_ratio
```
